title: --build-args do not work as expected within docker "ADD" blocks :  buildah version 1.11.2
body:
Having updated to version 1.11.2 of buildah the build args in a dockerfile do not resolve to their correct value in ADD blocks. I have created a minimal repro as follows


1. Create a file called abc.txt
2. Create a dockerfile as follows
```Dockerfile
FROM centos
ARG testArg
ADD ${testArg}.txt .
```
3. Run the following command
```bash
buildah bud --build-arg testArg=abc
```

You should receive the following output
```
STEP 1: FROM centos
STEP 2: ARG testArg
STEP 3: ADD ${testArg}.txt .
error dry-running "ADD ${testArg}.txt .": no files found matching "/home/tiernemi/buildah_test/${testArg}.txt": no such file or directory
ERRO exit status 1                                
```

When running the following command with docker
```bash
docker build . --build-arg testArg=abc
```
It succeeds with no issue.

comments.body: @tiernemi Thanks for the issue, especially since you've a nice reproducer.  Will take a look.

@tiernemi I'm able to reproduce and it looks like the ARG part of things are working appropriately as I'm able to do `RUN echo $testArg` just before the `ADD` line and that resolves OK.  Do you by chance know if this worked in earlier versions of Buildah?
It was working using the previous version of buildah in our pipeline. Version 1.10.1-2.
Thanks @tiernemi, I think I've found a PR that went into 1.11.0 that may have caused the issue, looking further.
we can close this now that #1874 merged?
@dustymabe yes we can!  I was holding off until we got a release together for Podman, but just verified this is in Buildah v1.11.3 which has been vendored upstream.  Thanks for the poke.
